title: HINCRBYFLOAT
description: Increments the floating point value of a field by a number. Uses 0 as initial value if the field doesn't exist.

sections.title: Redis Software and Redis Cloud compatibility

content:
Increment the specified `field` of a hash stored at `key`, and representing a floating point number, by the specified `increment`. If the increment value is negative, the result is to have the hash field value **decremented** instead of incremented. If the field does not exist, it is set to `0` before performing the operation. An error is returned if one of the following conditions occur: * The key contains a value of the wrong type (not a hash). * The current field content or the specified increment are not parsable as a double precision floating point number. The exact behavior of this command is identical to the one of the [`INCRBYFLOAT`]() command, please refer to the documentation of [`INCRBYFLOAT`]() for further information. ## Examples HSET mykey field 10.50 HINCRBYFLOAT mykey field 0.1 HINCRBYFLOAT mykey field -5 HSET mykey field 5.0e3 HINCRBYFLOAT mykey field 2.0e2 ## Implementation details The command is always propagated in the replication link and the Append Only File as a [`HSET`]() operation, so that differences in the underlying floating point math implementation will not be sources of inconsistency. ## Redis Software and Redis Cloud compatibility | Redis
Software | Redis
Cloud | Notes | |:----------------------|:-----------------|:------| | ✅ Standard
✅ Active-Active | ✅ Standard
✅ Active-Active | | ## Return information **RESP2:** [Bulk string reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#bulk-strings): the value of the field after the increment operation. **RESP3:** [Bulk string reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#bulk-strings): The value of the field after the increment operation.
intro:
Increment the specified `field` of a hash stored at `key`, and representing a
floating point number, by the specified `increment`. If the increment value
is negative, the result is to have the hash field value **decremented** instead of incremented.
If the field does not exist, it is set to `0` before performing the operation.
An error is returned if one of the following conditions occur:

* The key contains a value of the wrong type (not a hash).
* The current field content or the specified increment are not parsable as a
  double precision floating point number.

The exact behavior of this command is identical to the one of the [`INCRBYFLOAT`]()
command, please refer to the documentation of [`INCRBYFLOAT`]() for further
information.

sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
Increment the specified `field` of a hash stored at `key`, and representing a
floating point number, by the specified `increment`. If the increment value
is negative, the result is to have the hash field value **decremented** instead of incremented.
If the field does not exist, it is set to `0` before performing the operation.
An error is returned if one of the following conditions occur:

* The key contains a value of the wrong type (not a hash).
* The current field content or the specified increment are not parsable as a
  double precision floating point number.

The exact behavior of this command is identical to the one of the [`INCRBYFLOAT`]()
command, please refer to the documentation of [`INCRBYFLOAT`]() for further
information.

sections.title: Examples
sections.content:
HSET mykey field 10.50
HINCRBYFLOAT mykey field 0.1
HINCRBYFLOAT mykey field -5
HSET mykey field 5.0e3
HINCRBYFLOAT mykey field 2.0e2

sections.title: Implementation details
sections.content:
The command is always propagated in the replication link and the Append Only
File as a [`HSET`]() operation, so that differences in the underlying floating point
math implementation will not be sources of inconsistency.

sections.title: Redis Software and Redis Cloud compatibility
sections.content:
| Redis
Software | Redis
Cloud | Notes | |:----------------------|:-----------------|:------| | ✅ Standard
✅ Active-Active | ✅ Standard
✅ Active-Active | |

sections.title: Return information
sections.content:
**RESP2:**

[Bulk string reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#bulk-strings): the value of the field after the increment operation.

**RESP3:**

[Bulk string reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#bulk-strings): The value of the field after the increment operation.
